name: Enterprise Multi-Stage Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master

jobs:
  # STAGE 1: Build the code
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      artifact-path: ./publish
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
      with:
        dotnet-version: '10.0.x'
    - name: Build & Publish
      run: |
        dotnet restore
        dotnet build --configuration Release
        dotnet publish --configuration Release --output ./publish
    # Temporarily save the compiled files so the next jobs can use them
    - name: Upload Artifact
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: dotnet-app
        path: ./publish

  # STAGE 2: Deploy to Staging Automatically (No Approvals)
    # STAGE 2: Deploy to Staging Automatically
  deploy-to-staging:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
    - name: Download Artifact
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: dotnet-app
        path: ./publish

    # 1. Log into Azure securely using your new Service Principal token
    - name: Azure Login
      uses: azure/login@v2
      with:
        creds: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CREDENTIALS }}

    # 2. Deploy directly to the web app using the active login session
    - name: Deploy to Azure Staging
      uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v3
      with:
        app-name: 'StagingHybridApp' # Replace with your staging app name
        package: ./publish

  # STAGE 3: Deploy to Production (Requires Manual Click)
  deploy-to-production:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: deploy-to-staging
    environment: Production
    steps:
    - name: Download Artifact
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: dotnet-app
        path: ./publish

    # 1. Log into Azure securely
    - name: Azure Login
      uses: azure/login@v2
      with:
        creds: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CREDENTIALS }}

    # 2. Deploy directly to Production
    - name: Deploy to Azure Production
      uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v3
      with:
        app-name: 'HybridApp' # Replace with your production app name
        package: ./publish
